_comment: Documented exceptions to tools/anti_drift.py. Each entry needs a 'why' and an owner. The gate honors entries that match BOTH file (suffix match) AND rule, optionally narrowed by 'contains'. The rule name 'p0-vocabulary' replaced the legacy 'forbidden-copy' in v0.8.1; both are accepted for backwards compatibility.
_post_p0_extension_pattern: When a post-P0 packet sanctions a vocabulary surface (combat, RPG progression, professions, etc.), add an entry here with rule='p0-vocabulary', file=<the post-P0 packet's directory suffix>, and a 'why' that names the sanctioning packet.

entries.why: errata documents the rename — listing the old term is required.
the lock spec lists the restricted P0 copy in a fenced block.
tests reference the restricted P0 vocabulary as anti-pattern strings.
aesthetic bible names the restricted P0 vocabulary in the negative manifesto.
legacy rule name; preserved for backwards compatibility.
legacy rule name; preserved for backwards compatibility.
aesthetic bible documents the Paradise sacred register.
locks file describes the P2 reservation in prose.
design delta documents the renames (QuestLog/Stats/nora_field/dr_bell/bitterleaf) as part of the v0.7→v0.8.1 migration table and the post-P0 expansion clauses.
asset manifest documents the canonical resource ids and their migration history (nora_finch from nora_field; dr_amos_bell from dr_bell).
scene bible names the migration in line with the v0.8.1 errata (Bitterleaf removal, etc.).
prompt pack references the migration vocabulary (Bitterleaf in the prior recipe context).
overview HTML displays the v0.8 → v0.8.1 migration/errata table by definition.
